context: Tests whether the agent uses the correct ElevenLabs JavaScript package (@elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js, not the deprecated elevenlabs package), imports ElevenLabsClient correctly, reads the API key from the environment, pipes audio output to a write stream, and avoids passing both prompt and composition_plan simultaneously.

checklist.name: Correct npm package
checklist.description: package.json lists @elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js as a dependency, NOT the deprecated 'elevenlabs' package

checklist.name: Correct import
checklist.description: Script imports ElevenLabsClient from '@elevenlabs/elevenlabs-js' (not from 'elevenlabs' or any other package)

checklist.name: Environment variable for API key
checklist.description: Client is instantiated without hardcoded API key — relies on ELEVENLABS_API_KEY environment variable (i.e. new ElevenLabsClient() with no apiKey argument, or reads process.env.ELEVENLABS_API_KEY)

checklist.name: Calls music.compose
checklist.description: Script calls client.music.compose() with a prompt argument

checklist.name: No dual prompt+plan
checklist.description: music.compose() call does NOT pass both a prompt and a composition_plan simultaneously

checklist.name: Duration in valid range
checklist.description: musicLengthMs (or music_length_ms) value passed to compose() is between 3000 and 600000 (inclusive)

checklist.name: Audio piped to write stream
checklist.description: The audio response is saved by piping it to a write stream (e.g. audio.pipe(createWriteStream(...))) rather than using a simple write() call on the raw response object

checklist.name: Output filename correct
checklist.description: The output file is named podcast-intro.mp3

checklist.name: CLI argument as prompt
checklist.description: The music description is read from a command-line argument (e.g. process.argv[2]) rather than hardcoded
